assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements, not separate or individual financial statements.
A declared intragroup dividend recorded as receivable/payable is analysed as a recognised intragroup monetary item for purposes of testing the IFRS 9 exception.

approaches.label: Foreign currency hedge of an intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 permits an intragroup monetary payable or receivable to be a hedged item in consolidation if its foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: General external-party hedged item rule
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 starts from the principle that hedged items in consolidated financial statements must relate to parties external to the reporting entity unless a stated exception applies.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of a forecast intragroup transaction
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 separately allows a highly probable forecast intragroup transaction to be hedged only when the foreign currency risk will affect consolidated profit or loss, which frames the treatment before a dividend is recognised.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 identifies a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk on net investments in foreign operations, which is distinct from hedging an intragroup dividend receivable.
